user_query:
I'm putting together a small, shareable social content pack to explain how I was wrongly denied my right to vote in Riverland because of outdated and discriminatory rules, and to back it up with citations. Using only the files under input/, please do the following and save the deliverables under the specified paths:

1) Extract facts into a structured fact list at output/extracted_facts.json. Requirements:
- Represent the facts as a JSON array of objects with fields: id (string), source_file (string), source_type (one of: testimony|law|data), key (string identifier such as a section id or a row key), text (concise 1-2 sentence fact), and citation (string).
- Include at least: (a) two testimony timeline facts (with explicit years) from input/testimony.txt, (b) two law facts from input/law_excerpt.html that capture the ambiguous clause (§12.4(b)) and the 2018 amendment (HB-88) clarifying restoration rules, and (c) two data facts from input/data/disenfranchisement.csv (one must be the Riverland 2020 figure).
- For the law facts, set key to the HTML section ids ("sec-12-4b" and "amendment-hb-88"). For data facts, set key to a combination of state and year (e.g., "Riverland-2020").

2) Draft exactly 6 social posts and save them to output/posts.json as a JSON array. Each post object must have: id (string), platform ("twitter" or "facebook"), theme (one of: personal_story|law_explainer|data_point), post_text (string), source_ids (array of string fact ids from output/extracted_facts.json), and hashtags (array of 2-5 strings).
- Platform mix: exactly 3 posts for twitter and 3 for facebook. Enforce that any twitter post_text is 280 characters or fewer.
- Theme mix: exactly 2 personal_story posts (each must reference at least one testimony fact id), 2 law_explainer posts (each must include the fact id with key "amendment-hb-88" and may also include the §12.4(b) fact), and 2 data_point posts (each must reference at least one data fact id). All posts must be grounded in the extracted facts via source_ids.
- Write posts in a respectful, rights-affirming tone. Where appropriate, include a clear call to action (e.g., check your rights, share this) and keep claims consistent with the cited facts.

3) Create a linkage table at output/coverage.csv with columns: post_id, theme, platform, source_id, source_type. Include one row per (post, referenced fact) pair so that the total number of rows equals the total count of all source_ids used across posts.

Validation constraints:
- Every source_id referenced in output/posts.json must exist in output/extracted_facts.json, and source_type in output/coverage.csv must match the corresponding fact.
- The extracted fact with key "amendment-hb-88" must be cited by both law_explainer posts.
- The Riverland 2020 data fact must be cited by at least one data_point post.
- Do not use the Internet; rely only on the provided local files. Keep all paths and filenames exactly as specified.

Input: input/testimony.txt, input/law_excerpt.html, input/data/disenfranchisement.csv
Outputs to create: output/extracted_facts.json, output/posts.json, output/coverage.csv All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
